title: TMNRED, A Chinese Language EEG Dataset for Fuzzy Semantic Target Identification in Natural Reading Environments
description: TMNRED is a Chinese language EEG dataset comprising recordings from 30 native Mandarin speakers during natural reading tasks designed to investigate semantic processing mechanisms. Participants read text stimuli (15-20 characters) including target and non-target semantic items presented at self-paced rates across 8 blocks of 400 trials each. Sensor-level analyses revealed distinct neural responses to target versus non-target words with notable scalp potential distribution changes, providing insights into fuzzy semantic target identification in naturalistic reading environments.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 30 healthy, right-handed participants (mean age 22.07 ± 2.7 years; 18 female, 12 male) during self-paced reading of Chinese text stimuli (15-20 characters) presented as news headlines or short sentences. Each participant completed 8 blocks of 400 trials with trial duration of approximately 2.2 seconds including fixation cross and inter-stimulus intervals. Sensor-level EEG analyses were performed to identify distinct responses to target and non-target words.

funding_references.funder_name: National Key R&D Program of China
National Natural Science Foundation of China
